points.text: Add detailed logging/timing around the checkout flow: record request start, DB query start/end, and each retry attempt to pinpoint latency sources.
Run EXPLAIN on the new join query, add missing indexes or rewrite the join to reduce execution time; verify query plans before and after changes.
Make the payment step idempotent: generate a unique order/payment token, enforce a DB unique constraint, and limit client retries to prevent double charges.
Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the checkout query to see join cost, row estimates, and missing indexes.
Wrap the entire checkout in one ACID transaction with SERIALIZABLE (or REPEATABLE READ) to avoid duplicate charges on retries.
Create indexes on the new join's foreign‑key columns and consider a covering index or query rewrite to reduce I/O.
Enable detailed DB query logging and profiling to measure the new join’s execution time and lock contention during checkout.
Wrap the payment charge in a single atomic transaction using a unique idempotency key to detect and reject duplicate attempts.
Add exponential backoff and a retry limit on the client, and enforce server‑side rate limiting to prevent rapid duplicate submissions.
